hypothesis: If thermodynamic data, cellular automata simulation, machine learning descriptors, and commercial database integration are combined into a unified computational workflow, then it will be possible to systematically identify cost-effective and technically feasible synthesis routes for inorganic materials such as Ca2SiO4 by simultaneously optimizing thermodynamic favorability, reaction probability, product quality, and reactant market price across a large chemical space, because integrating these complementary computational approaches captures the physical, chemical, and economic constraints that collectively determine practical synthesis viability.
